127	    def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
128	        if sampling_params.dtype is None:
129	            return
130	
131	        if sampling_params.stop == ():
132	            sampling_params.stop = []
133	
134	        dtype_regex = None
135	        if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
136	
137	            dtype_regex = REGEX_INT
138	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
139	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
140	
141	            dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT
142	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
143	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
144	
145	            dtype_regex = REGEX_STR
146	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
147	
148	            dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL
149	        else:
150	            raise RuntimeError(f"Invalid dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}")
151	
152	        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
153	            warnings.warn(
154	                f"Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used. dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}, regex: {sampling_params.regex}"
155	            )
156	
157	        sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
158	
159	    def generate(
160	        self,
161	        s: StreamExecutor,
162	        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
163	    ):
164	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
165	        data = {
166	            "text": s.text_,
167	            "sampling_params": {
168	                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
169	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
170	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
171	            },
172	        }
173	
174	        for item in [
175	            "return_logprob",
176	            "logprob_start_len",
177	            "top_logprobs_num",
178	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
179	        ]:
180	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
181	            if value is not None:
182	                data[item] = value
183	
184	        self._add_images(s, data)
185	
186	        res = http_request(
187	            self.base_url + "/generate",
188	            json=data,
189	            api_key=self.api_key,
190	            verify=self.verify,
191	        )
192	        self._assert_success(res)
193	
194	        obj = res.json()
195	        comp = obj["text"]
196	        return comp, obj["meta_info"]
197	
198	    def generate_stream(
199	        self,
200	        s: StreamExecutor,
201	        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
202	    ):
203	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
204	
205	        data = {
206	            "text": s.text_,
207	            "sampling_params": {
208	                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
209	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
210	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
211	            },
212	        }
213	
214	        for item in [
215	            "return_logprob",
216	            "logprob_start_len",
217	            "top_logprobs_num",
218	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
219	        ]:
220	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
221	            if value is not None:
222	                data[item] = value
223	
224	        data["stream"] = True
225	        self._add_images(s, data)
226	
227	        res = http_request(
228	            self.base_url + "/generate",
229	            json=data,
230	            stream=True,
231	            api_key=self.api_key,
232	            verify=self.verify,
233	        )
234	        self._assert_success(res)
235	        pos = 0
236	
237	        for chunk in res.iter_lines(decode_unicode=False):
238	            chunk = chunk.decode("utf-8")
239	            if chunk and chunk.startswith("data:"):
240	                if chunk == "data: [DONE]":
241	                    break
242	                data = json.loads(chunk[5:].strip("\n"))
243	                chunk_text = data["text"][pos:]
244	                meta_info = data["meta_info"]
245	                pos += len(chunk_text)
246	                yield chunk_text, meta_info
247	
248	    def select(
249	        self,
250	        s: StreamExecutor,
251	        choices: List[str],
252	        temperature: float,
253	        choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod,
254	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
255	        assert temperature <= 1e-5
256	
257	        # Cache common prefix
258	        data = {"text": s.text_, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}}
259	        obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
260	        prompt_len = obj["meta_info"]["prompt_tokens"]
261	        logprob_start_len = max(prompt_len - 2, 0)  # For token healing
262	
263	        # Compute logprob
264	        data = {
265	            "text": [s.text_ + c for c in choices],
266	            "sampling_params": {
267	                "max_new_tokens": 0,
268	                "temperature": 0,
269	            },
270	            "return_logprob": True,
271	            "return_text_in_logprobs": True,
272	            "logprob_start_len": logprob_start_len,
273	        }
274	        obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
275	
276	        input_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
277	        output_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["output_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
278	        normalized_prompt_logprobs = [
279	            compute_normalized_prompt_logprobs(r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"])
280	            for r in obj
281	        ]
282	
283	        # Remove extra token if no token healing occurred
284	        for i in range(len(input_token_logprobs)):
285	            healed_token_str = input_token_logprobs[i][0][-1]
286	            if s.text_.endswith(healed_token_str):
287	                healed_token_logprob = input_token_logprobs[i][0][0]
288	                normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] = (
289	                    normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] * len(input_token_logprobs[i])
290	                    - healed_token_logprob
291	                ) / (len(input_token_logprobs[i]) - 1)
292	                input_token_logprobs[i] = input_token_logprobs[i][1:]
293	
294	        # Compute unconditional logprobs if required
295	        if choices_method.requires_unconditional_logprobs:
296	            input_ids = [[el[1] for el in subl] for subl in input_token_logprobs]
297	            data = {
298	                "input_ids": input_ids,
299	                "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0},
300	                "return_logprob": True,
301	            }
302	            obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
303	            unconditional_token_logprobs = [
304	                r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj
305	            ]
306	        else:
307	            unconditional_token_logprobs = None
308	
309	        return choices_method(
310	            choices=choices,
311	            normalized_prompt_logprobs=normalized_prompt_logprobs,
312	            input_token_logprobs=input_token_logprobs,
313	            output_token_logprobs=output_token_logprobs,
314	            unconditional_token_logprobs=unconditional_token_logprobs,
315	        )
316	
317	    def concatenate_and_append(self, src_rids: List[str], dst_rid: str):
318	        res = http_request(
319	            self.base_url + "/concate_and_append_request",
320	            json={"src_rids": src_rids, "dst_rid": dst_rid},
321	            api_key=self.api_key,
322	            verify=self.verify,
323	        )
324	        self._assert_success(res)
325	
326	    def _generate_http_request(self, s: StreamExecutor, data):
327	        self._add_images(s, data)
328	        res = http_request(
329	            self.base_url + "/generate",
330	            json=data,
331	            api_key=self.api_key,
332	            verify=self.verify,
333	        )
334	        self._assert_success(res)
335	        return res.json()
336	
337	    def _add_images(self, s: StreamExecutor, data):
338	        if s.images_:
339	            assert len(s.images_) == 1, "Only support one image."
340	            data["image_data"] = s.images_[0][1]
341	
342	    def _assert_success(self, res):
343	        if res.status_code != 200:
344	            try:
345	                content = res.json()
346	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
347	                content = res.text
348	            raise RuntimeError(content)
349	
350	
351	def compute_normalized_prompt_logprobs(input_logprobs):
352	    values = [x[0] for x in input_logprobs if x[0]]
353	    return sum(values) / len(values)

356	class Runtime:
357	    """
358	    A wrapper for the HTTP server.
359	    This is used for launching the server in a python program without
360	    using the command line interface.
361	
362	    It is mainly used for the frontend language.
363	    You should use the Engine class if you want to do normal offline processing without the frontend language.
364	    """
365	
366	    def __init__(
367	        self,
368	        log_level: str = "error",
369	        launch_timeout: float = 300.0,
370	        *args,
371	        **kwargs,
372	    ):
373	        """See the arguments in server_args.py::ServerArgs
374	
375	        Args:
376	            log_level: Log level for the server.
377	            timeout: Timeout in seconds for waiting for the server to start.
378	            *args: Additional arguments passed to ServerArgs.
379	            **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to ServerArgs.
380	        """
381	        # We delay the import of any `sglang.srt` components in `sglang.lang`, so users can run
382	        # client code without installing SRT server and its dependency if they want.
383	        from sglang.srt.entrypoints.http_server import launch_server
384	        from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs
385	        from sglang.srt.utils import is_port_available
386	
387	        self.server_args = ServerArgs(*args, log_level=log_level, **kwargs)
388	
389	        # Pre-allocate ports
390	        for port in range(self.server_args.port, 40000):
391	            if is_port_available(port):
392	                break
393	        self.server_args.port = port
394	
395	        self.url = self.server_args.url()
396	        self.generate_url = self.url + "/generate"
397	
398	        # NOTE: We store pid instead of proc to fix some issues during __delete__
399	        self.pid = None

199	async def init_multi_tokenizer() -> ServerArgs:
200	    """
201	    Initialization function for multi-process tokenizer mode.
202	    It read args information from shm and inits tokenizer manager for current process.
203	    """
204	
205	    # Read configuration from shared memory
206	    main_pid = get_main_process_id()
207	    port_args, server_args, scheduler_info = read_from_shared_memory(
208	        f"multi_tokenizer_args_{main_pid}"
209	    )
210	    server_args: ServerArgs
211	    port_args: PortArgs
212	
213	    # API key authentication is not supported in multi-tokenizer mode
214	    assert (
215	        server_args.api_key is None
216	    ), "API key is not supported in multi-tokenizer mode"
217	
218	    # Create a new ipc name for the current process
219	    port_args.tokenizer_ipc_name = (
220	        f"ipc://{tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False).name}"
221	    )
222	    logger.info(
223	        f"Start multi-tokenizer worker process {os.getpid()}, "
224	        f"ipc_name={port_args.tokenizer_ipc_name}"
225	    )
226	
227	    # Launch multi-tokenizer manager process
228	    tokenizer_manager = TokenizerWorker(server_args, port_args)
229	    template_manager = TemplateManager()
230	    template_manager.initialize_templates(
231	        tokenizer_manager=tokenizer_manager,
232	        model_path=server_args.model_path,
233	        chat_template=server_args.chat_template,
234	        completion_template=server_args.completion_template,
235	    )
236	
237	    tokenizer_manager.max_req_input_len = scheduler_info["max_req_input_len"]
238	
239	    set_global_state(
240	        _GlobalState(
241	            tokenizer_manager=tokenizer_manager,
242	            template_manager=template_manager,
243	            scheduler_info=scheduler_info,
244	        )
245	    )
246	
247	    return server_args

251	async def lifespan(fast_api_app: FastAPI):
252	    if getattr(fast_api_app, "is_single_tokenizer_mode", False):
253	        server_args = fast_api_app.server_args
254	        warmup_thread_kwargs = fast_api_app.warmup_thread_kwargs
255	        thread_label = "Tokenizer"
256	    else:
257	        # Initialize multi-tokenizer support for worker processes
258	        server_args = await init_multi_tokenizer()
259	        warmup_thread_kwargs = dict(server_args=server_args)
260	        thread_label = f"MultiTokenizer-{_global_state.tokenizer_manager.worker_id}"
261	
262	    # Add prometheus middleware
263	    if server_args.enable_metrics:
264	        add_prometheus_middleware(app)
265	        enable_func_timer()
266	
267	    # Init tracing
268	    if server_args.enable_trace:
269	        process_tracing_init(server_args.otlp_traces_endpoint, "sglang")
270	        if server_args.disaggregation_mode == "prefill":
271	            thread_label = "Prefill" + thread_label
272	        elif server_args.disaggregation_mode == "decode":
273	            thread_label = "Decode" + thread_label
274	        trace_set_thread_info(thread_label)
275	
276	    # Initialize OpenAI serving handlers
277	    fast_api_app.state.openai_serving_completion = OpenAIServingCompletion(
278	        _global_state.tokenizer_manager, _global_state.template_manager
279	    )
280	    fast_api_app.state.openai_serving_chat = OpenAIServingChat(
281	        _global_state.tokenizer_manager, _global_state.template_manager
282	    )
283	    fast_api_app.state.openai_serving_embedding = OpenAIServingEmbedding(
284	        _global_state.tokenizer_manager, _global_state.template_manager
285	    )
286	    fast_api_app.state.openai_serving_classify = OpenAIServingClassify(
287	        _global_state.tokenizer_manager, _global_state.template_manager
288	    )
289	    fast_api_app.state.openai_serving_score = OpenAIServingScore(
290	        _global_state.tokenizer_manager
291	    )
292	    fast_api_app.state.openai_serving_rerank = OpenAIServingRerank(
293	        _global_state.tokenizer_manager, _global_state.template_manager
294	    )
295	    fast_api_app.state.openai_serving_tokenize = OpenAIServingTokenize(
296	        _global_state.tokenizer_manager
297	    )
298	    fast_api_app.state.openai_serving_detokenize = OpenAIServingDetokenize(
299	        _global_state.tokenizer_manager
300	    )
301	
302	    # Initialize Ollama-compatible serving handler
303	    fast_api_app.state.ollama_serving = OllamaServing(_global_state.tokenizer_manager)
304	
305	    # Initialize Anthropic-compatible serving handler
306	    fast_api_app.state.anthropic_serving = AnthropicServing(
307	        fast_api_app.state.openai_serving_chat
308	    )
309	
310	    # Launch tool server
311	    tool_server = None
312	    if server_args.tool_server == "demo":
313	        from sglang.srt.entrypoints.openai.tool_server import DemoToolServer
314	
315	        tool_server = DemoToolServer()
316	    elif server_args.tool_server:
317	        from sglang.srt.entrypoints.openai.tool_server import MCPToolServer
318	
319	        tool_server = MCPToolServer()
320	        await tool_server.add_tool_server(server_args.tool_server)
321	
322	    try:
323	        from sglang.srt.entrypoints.openai.serving_responses import (
324	            OpenAIServingResponses,
325	        )
326	
327	        fast_api_app.state.openai_serving_responses = OpenAIServingResponses(
328	            _global_state.tokenizer_manager,
329	            _global_state.template_manager,
330	            enable_prompt_tokens_details=True,
331	            enable_force_include_usage=True,
332	            tool_server=tool_server,
333	        )
334	    except Exception:
335	        traceback = get_exception_traceback()
336	        logger.warning(f"Can not initialize OpenAIServingResponses, error: {traceback}")
337	
338	    # Execute custom warmups
339	    if server_args.warmups is not None:
340	        await execute_warmups(
341	            server_args.disaggregation_mode,
342	            server_args.warmups.split(","),
343	            _global_state.tokenizer_manager,
344	        )
345	        logger.info("Warmup ended")
346	
347	    # Execute the general warmup
348	    warmup_thread = threading.Thread(
349	        target=_wait_and_warmup,
350	        kwargs=warmup_thread_kwargs,
351	    )
352	    warmup_thread.start()
353	
354	    # Start the HTTP server
355	    try:
356	        yield
357	    finally:
358	        warmup_thread.join()
359

365	)

372	)

380	@app.exception_handler(HTTPException)
381	async def validation_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: HTTPException):
382	    """Enrich HTTP exception with status code and other details.
383	
384	    For /v1/responses, emit OpenAI-style nested error envelope:
385	    {"error": {"message": "...", "type": "...", "param": null, "code": <status>}}
386	    """
387	    # adjust fmt for responses api
388	    if request.url.path.startswith("/v1/responses"):
389	        nested_error = {
390	            "message": exc.detail,
391	            "type": HTTPStatus(exc.status_code).phrase,
392	            "param": None,
393	            "code": exc.status_code,
394	        }
395	        return ORJSONResponse(
396	            content={"error": nested_error}, status_code=exc.status_code
397	        )
398	
399	    error = ErrorResponse(
400	        object="error",
401	        message=exc.detail,
402	        type=str(exc.status_code),
403	        code=exc.status_code,
404	    )
405	    return ORJSONResponse(content=error.model_dump(), status_code=exc.status_code)
406	
407	
408	# Custom exception handlers to change validation error status codes
409	@app.exception_handler(RequestValidationError)
410	async def validation_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: RequestValidationError):
411	    """Override FastAPI's default 422 validation error with 400.
412	
413	    For /v1/responses, emit OpenAI-style nested error envelope; for other endpoints keep legacy format.
414	    """
415	    exc_str = str(exc)
416	    errors_str = str(exc.errors())
417	
418	    if errors_str and errors_str != exc_str:
419	        message = f"{exc_str} {errors_str}"
420	    else:
421	        message = exc_str
422	
423	    if request.url.path.startswith("/v1/responses"):
424	        # adapt specially, for v1/responses API only (notice the error key is different)
425	        nested_error = {
426	            "message": message,
427	            "type": HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST.phrase,
428	            "param": None,
429	            "code": HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST.value,
430	        }
431	        return ORJSONResponse(status_code=400, content={"error": nested_error})
432	
433	    err = ErrorResponse(
434	        message=message,
435	        type=HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST.phrase,
436	        code=HTTPStatus.BAD_REQUEST.value,
437	    )
438	
439	    return ORJSONResponse(
440	        status_code=400,
441	        content=err.model_dump(),
442	    )

445	async def validate_json_request(raw_request: Request):
446	    """Validate that the request content-type is application/json."""
447	    content_type = raw_request.headers.get("content-type", "").lower()
448	    media_type = content_type.split(";", maxsplit=1)[0]
449	    if media_type != "application/json":
450	        raise RequestValidationError(
451	            errors=[
452	                {
453	                    "loc": ["header", "content-type"],
454	                    "msg": "Unsupported Media Type: Only 'application/json' is allowed",
455	                    "type": "value_error",
456	                }
457	            ]
458	        )

464	@app.get("/health")
465	@app.get("/health_generate")
466	async def health_generate(request: Request) -> Response:
467	    """
468	    Check the health of the inference server by sending a special request to generate one token.
469	
470	    If the server is running something, this request will be ignored, so it creates zero overhead.
471	    If the server is not running anything, this request will be run, so we know whether the server is healthy.
472	    """
473	
474	    if _global_state.tokenizer_manager.gracefully_exit:
475	        logger.info("Health check request received during shutdown. Returning 503.")
476	        return Response(status_code=503)
477	
478	    if _global_state.tokenizer_manager.server_status == ServerStatus.Starting:
479	        return Response(status_code=503)
480	
481	    if (
482	        not envs.SGLANG_ENABLE_HEALTH_ENDPOINT_GENERATION.get()
483	        and request.url.path == "/health"
484	    ):
485	        return Response(status_code=200)
486	
487	    sampling_params = {"max_new_tokens": 1, "temperature": 0.0}
488	    rid = f"HEALTH_CHECK_{time.time()}"
489	
490	    if _global_state.tokenizer_manager.is_image_gen:
491	        gri = _global_state.tokenizer_manager.get_image_gen_health_check_request(
492	            rid, sampling_params
493	        )
494	    elif _global_state.tokenizer_manager.is_generation:
495	        gri = GenerateReqInput(
496	            rid=rid,
497	            input_ids=[0],
498	            sampling_params=sampling_params,
499	            log_metrics=False,
500	        )
501	        if (
502	            _global_state.tokenizer_manager.server_args.disaggregation_mode
503	            != DisaggregationMode.NULL.value
504	        ):
505	            gri.bootstrap_host = FAKE_BOOTSTRAP_HOST
506	            gri.bootstrap_room = 0
507	    else:
508	        gri = EmbeddingReqInput(
509	            rid=rid, input_ids=[0], sampling_params=sampling_params, log_metrics=False
510	        )
511	
512	    async def gen():
513	        async for _ in _global_state.tokenizer_manager.generate_request(gri, request):
514	            break
515	
516	    task = asyncio.create_task(gen())
517	
518	    # As long as we receive any response from the detokenizer/scheduler, we consider the server is healthy.
519	    tic = time.time()
520	    while time.time() < tic + HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT:
521	        await asyncio.sleep(1)
522	        if _global_state.tokenizer_manager.last_receive_tstamp > tic:
523	            task.cancel()
524	            _global_state.tokenizer_manager.rid_to_state.pop(rid, None)
525	            _global_state.tokenizer_manager.server_status = ServerStatus.Up
526	            return Response(status_code=200)
527	
528	    task.cancel()
529	    tic_time = time.strftime("%H:%M:%S", time.localtime(tic))
530	    last_receive_time = time.strftime(
531	        "%H:%M:%S", time.localtime(_global_state.tokenizer_manager.last_receive_tstamp)
532	    )
533	    logger.error(
534	        f"Health check failed. Server couldn't get a response from detokenizer for last "
535	        f"{HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT} seconds. tic start time: {tic_time}. "
536	        f"last_heartbeat time: {last_receive_time}"
537	    )
538	    _global_state.tokenizer_manager.rid_to_state.pop(rid, None)
539	    _global_state.tokenizer_manager.server_status = ServerStatus.UnHealthy
540	    return Response(status_code=503)

14	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
15	
16	import logging
17	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union
18	
19	# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
20	try:
21	    import re._parser as sre_parse
22	except ImportError:
23	    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11
24	
25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	
31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):

42	def run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync):
43	    try:
44	        state.ret_value = program.func(state, *func_args, **func_kwargs)
45	    except Exception as e:
46	        raise e
47	    finally:
48	        state.stream_executor.end()
49	
50	    if sync:
51	        state.stream_executor.sync()
52	
53	    if global_config.verbosity >= 2:
54	        print(state.text())

57	def run_program(
58	    program,
59	    backend,
60	    func_args,
61	    func_kwargs,
62	    default_sampling_para,
63	    stream,
64	    sync=False,
65	    use_thread=True,
66	):
67	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
68	        backend = backend.endpoint
69	    assert backend is not None, "Please specify a backend"
70	    func_kwargs.update(program.bind_arguments)
71	    stream_executor = StreamExecutor(
72	        backend,
73	        func_kwargs,
74	        default_sampling_para,
75	        chat_template=None,
76	        stream=stream,
77	        num_api_spec_tokens=program.num_api_spec_tokens,
78	        use_thread=use_thread,
79	    )
80	    state = ProgramState(stream_executor)
81	
82	    if stream:
83	        t = threading.Thread(
84	            target=run_internal, args=(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
85	        )
86	        t.start()
87	        return state
88	    else:
89	        run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
90	        return state

650	async def generate_request(obj: GenerateReqInput, request: Request):
651	    """Handle a generate request."""
652	    if obj.stream:
653	
654	        async def stream_results() -> AsyncIterator[bytes]:
655	            try:
656	                async for out in _global_state.tokenizer_manager.generate_request(
657	                    obj, request
658	                ):
659	                    yield b"data: " + orjson.dumps(
660	                        out, option=orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS
661	                    ) + b"\n\n"
662	            except ValueError as e:
663	                out = {"error": {"message": str(e)}}
664	                logger.error(f"[http_server] Error: {e}")
665	                yield b"data: " + orjson.dumps(
666	                    out, option=orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS
667	                ) + b"\n\n"
668	            yield b"data: [DONE]\n\n"
669	
670	        return StreamingResponse(
671	            stream_results(),
672	            media_type="text/event-stream",
673	            background=_global_state.tokenizer_manager.create_abort_task(obj),
674	        )
675	    else:
676	        try:
677	            ret = await _global_state.tokenizer_manager.generate_request(
678	                obj, request
679	            ).__anext__()
680	            return ret
681	        except ValueError as e:
682	            logger.error(f"[http_server] Error: {e}")
683	            return _create_error_response(e)

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

68	@dataclass
69	class RequestTimingMetricsMixin:
70	    """
71	    Mixin class containing common request-level timing metrics.
72	
73	    This class consolidates the timing metrics that are shared across all batch output types
74	    to avoid code duplication and ensure consistency.
75	    """
76	
77	    # Queue duration: time spent waiting in queue before request is scheduled.
78	    queue_time: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
79	
80	    # Forward entry time: timestamp when the request enters the forward pass stage.
81	    # This corresponds to `forward_entry_time` in TimeStats.
82	    # In different modes:
83	    #   - Unified/PD-colocate: timestamp when forward computation begins (covers prefill + decode)
84	    #   - Prefill instance (P): timestamp when prefill forward pass begins
85	    #   - Decode instance (D): timestamp when decode forward pass begins
86	    # Note: This is NOT the same as prefill_start_time. There may be a delay between
87	    # forward_entry_time and prefill_start_time (see prefill_launch_delay).
88	    forward_entry_time: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
89	
90	    # Prefill launch delay: time spent waiting between forward entry and prefill start.
91	    # Calculated as: prefill_start_time - forward_entry_time
92	    # This represents the delay between when the request enters the forward stage
93	    # and when prefill computation actually begins.
94	    prefill_launch_delay: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
95	
96	    # Prefill launch latency: time spent during prefill kernel launch.
97	    # Calculated as: prefill_end_time_host - prefill_start_time_host
98	    prefill_launch_latency: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
99	
100	    # Prefill finished time: timestamp when prefill phase completes (wall clock time).
101	    # This marks when the prefill computation finishes.
102	    prefill_finished_ts: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
103	
104	
105	@dataclass
106	class SpeculativeDecodingMetricsMixin:
107	    """
108	    Mixin class containing speculative decoding metrics.
109	
110	    This class consolidates speculative decoding metrics that are shared across
111	    batch output types that support speculative decoding to avoid code duplication.
112	    """
113	
114	    # Verify count: number of verification forward passes
115	    spec_verify_ct: List[int]
116	
117	    # Accepted tokens: Number of accepted tokens during speculative decoding
118	    spec_accepted_tokens: List[int]
119	
120	    # Acceptance histogram: List of lists, where each inner list represents histogram counts.
121	    # List index = number of accepted tokens in a step, List value = count of steps with that many accepted tokens.
122	    # Example: histogram[0] = 5 means 5 steps with 0 accepted tokens, histogram[3] = 10 means 10 steps with 3 accepted tokens.
123	    # Empty list [] when speculative decoding is disabled.
124	    spec_acceptance_histogram: List[List[int]]

289	    def normalize_batch_and_arguments(self):
290	        """
291	        Normalize the batch size and arguments for the request.
292	
293	        This method resolves various input formats and ensures all parameters
294	        are properly formatted as either single values or batches depending on the input.
295	        It also handles parallel sampling expansion and sets default values for
296	        unspecified parameters.
297	
298	        Raises:
299	            ValueError: If inputs are not properly specified (e.g., none or all of
300	                       text, input_ids, input_embeds are provided)
301	        """
302	        self._validate_inputs()
303	        self._determine_batch_size()
304	        self._handle_parallel_sampling()
305	
306	        if self.is_single:
307	            self._normalize_single_inputs()
308	        else:
309	            self._normalize_batch_inputs()
310	
311	    def _validate_inputs(self):
312	        """Validate that the input configuration is valid."""
313	        if (
314	            self.text is None and self.input_ids is None and self.input_embeds is None
315	        ) or (
316	            self.text is not None
317	            and self.input_ids is not None
318	            and self.input_embeds is not None
319	        ):
320	            raise ValueError(
321	                "Either text, input_ids or input_embeds should be provided."
322	            )
323	
324	    def _determine_batch_size(self):
325	        """Determine if this is a single example or a batch and the batch size."""
326	        if self.text is not None:
327	            if isinstance(self.text, str):
328	                self.is_single = True
329	                self.batch_size = 1
330	            else:
331	                self.is_single = False
332	                self.batch_size = len(self.text)
333	            self.input_embeds = None
334	        elif self.input_ids is not None:
335	            if len(self.input_ids) == 0:
336	                raise ValueError("input_ids cannot be empty.")
337	            if isinstance(self.input_ids[0], int):
338	                self.is_single = True
339	                self.batch_size = 1
340	            else:
341	                self.is_single = False
342	                self.batch_size = len(self.input_ids)
343	            self.input_embeds = None
344	        else:
345	            if isinstance(self.input_embeds[0][0], float):
346	                self.is_single = True
347	                self.batch_size = 1
348	            else:
349	                self.is_single = False
350	                self.batch_size = len(self.input_embeds)
351	
352	    def _handle_parallel_sampling(self):
353	        """Handle parallel sampling parameters and adjust batch size if needed."""
354	        # Determine parallel sample count
355	        if self.sampling_params is None:
356	            self.parallel_sample_num = 1
357	            return
358	        elif isinstance(self.sampling_params, dict):
359	            self.parallel_sample_num = self.sampling_params.get("n", 1)
360	        else:  # isinstance(self.sampling_params, list):
361	            self.parallel_sample_num = self.sampling_params[0].get("n", 1)
362	            for sampling_params in self.sampling_params:
363	                if self.parallel_sample_num != sampling_params.get("n", 1):
364	                    raise ValueError(
365	                        "The parallel_sample_num should be the same for all samples in sample params."
366	                    )
367	
368	        # If using parallel sampling with a single example, convert to batch
369	        if self.parallel_sample_num > 1 and self.is_single:
370	            self.is_single = False
371	            if self.text is not None:
372	                self.text = [self.text]
373	            if self.input_ids is not None:
374	                self.input_ids = [self.input_ids]
375	            if self.input_embeds is not None:
376	                self.input_embeds = [self.input_embeds]
377	
378	    def _normalize_single_inputs(self):
379	        """Normalize inputs for a single example."""
380	        if self.sampling_params is None:
381	            self.sampling_params = {}
382	        if self.rid is None:
383	            self.rid = uuid.uuid4().hex
384	        if self.return_logprob is None:
385	            self.return_logprob = False
386	        if self.logprob_start_len is None:
387	            self.logprob_start_len = -1
388	        if self.top_logprobs_num is None:
389	            self.top_logprobs_num = 0
390	        if not self.token_ids_logprob:  # covers both None and []
391	            self.token_ids_logprob = None
392	
393	    def _normalize_batch_inputs(self):
394	        """Normalize inputs for a batch of examples, including parallel sampling expansion."""
395	        # Calculate expanded batch size
396	        if self.parallel_sample_num == 1:
397	            num = self.batch_size
398	        else:
399	            # Expand parallel_sample_num
400	            num = self.batch_size * self.parallel_sample_num

69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.
155	        penalizer_orchestrator = penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator(
156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )
164	
165	        ret = cls(
166	            temperatures=temperatures,
167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)
184	        return ret

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs

32	    compilation_time: Optional[float] = None
33	    schema_count: Optional[int] = None
34	    ebnf_size: Optional[int] = None
35	    is_cache_hit: bool = False
36	    is_grammar_aborted: bool = False

42	class BaseGrammarObject:
43	
44	    def __init__(self):
45	        self._finished = False
46	        self.grammar_stats = None
47	        self.current_token = None
48	
49	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
50	        pass
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
53	        """
54	        Accept a token in the grammar.
55	        """
56	        raise NotImplementedError()
57	
58	    def rollback(self, k: int):
59	        raise NotImplementedError()
60	
61	    def is_terminated(self):
62	        return False
63	
64	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
65	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
66	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
70	        raise NotImplementedError()
71	
72	    @staticmethod
73	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
74	        raise NotImplementedError()
75	
76	    @staticmethod
77	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
78	        raise NotImplementedError()
79	
80	    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
81	        return self
82	
83	    @property
84	    def finished(self):
85	        return self._finished
86	
87	    @finished.setter
88	    def finished(self, finished):
89	        self._finished = finished
90	
91	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
92	        """
93	        Try to jump forward in the grammar.
94	
95	        Returns:
96	            A jump forward helper which may be used in `jump_forward_str_state`.
97	            None if the jump forward is not possible.
98	        """
99	        raise NotImplementedError()
100	
101	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
102	        """
103	        Jump forward for the grammar.
104	
105	        Returns:
106	            A tuple of the jump forward string and the next state of the grammar
107	            (which can be used in `jump_and_retokenize` if needed).
108	        """
109	        raise NotImplementedError()
110	
111	    def jump_and_retokenize(
112	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
113	    ) -> None:
114	        """
115	        Jump forward occurs, and update the grammar state if needed.
116	        """
117	        raise NotImplementedError()

123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197

199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

87	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
88	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
89	    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
90	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
91	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
92	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
93	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
94	    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
95	    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
96	    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
97	    regex: Optional[str] = None,
98	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
99	):
100	    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
101	
102	    if choices:
103	        return SglSelect(
104	            name,
105	            choices,
106	            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
107	            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
108	        )
109	
110	    # check regex is valid
111	    if regex is not None:
112	        try:
113	            re.compile(regex)
114	        except re.error as e:
115	            raise e
116	
117	    return SglGen(
118	        name,
119	        max_tokens,
120	        min_tokens,
121	        n,

124	        stop_regex,

132	        return_logprob,
133	        logprob_start_len,
134	        top_logprobs_num,
135	        return_text_in_logprobs,
136	        dtype,
137	        regex,
138	        json_schema,
139	    )

17	@dataclasses.dataclass
18	class SglSamplingParams:
19	    max_new_tokens: int = 128
20	    min_new_tokens: int = 0
21	    n: int = 1
22	    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
23	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
24	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
25	    temperature: float = 1.0
26	    top_p: float = 1.0
27	    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
28	    min_p: float = 0.0
29	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
30	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
31	    ignore_eos: bool = False
32	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
33	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
34	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
35	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
36	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
39	    dtype: Optional[str] = None
40	    regex: Optional[str] = None
41	
42	    def clone(self):
43	        return SglSamplingParams(
44	            self.max_new_tokens,
45	            self.min_new_tokens,
46	            self.n,
47	            self.stop,
48	            self.stop_token_ids,
49	            self.stop_regex,
50	            self.temperature,
51	            self.top_p,
52	            self.top_k,
53	            self.min_p,
54	            self.frequency_penalty,
55	            self.presence_penalty,
56	            self.ignore_eos,
57	            self.return_logprob,
58	            self.logprob_start_len,
59	            self.top_logprobs_num,
60	            self.return_text_in_logprobs,
61	            self.json_schema,
62	        )
63	
64	    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
65	        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
66	        if self.regex is not None:
67	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,
72	            "stop": self.stop or None,
73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
74	            "top_p": self.top_p,
75	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
76	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
77	        }
78	
79	    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
80	        if self.regex is not None:
81	            warnings.warn(
82	                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
83	            )
84	        return {
85	            "candidate_count": 1,
86	            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
87	            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
88	            "temperature": self.temperature,
89	            "top_p": self.top_p,
90	            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
91	        }
92	
93	    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
94	        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
95	        if self.regex is not None:
96	            warnings.warn(
97	                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
98	            )
99	        return {
100	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
101	            "stop_sequences": (
102	                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]
103	            ),
104	            "temperature": self.temperature,
105	            "top_p": self.top_p,
106	            "top_k": self.top_k,
107	        }
108	
109	    def to_litellm_kwargs(self):
110	        if self.regex is not None:
111	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the LiteLLM backend.")
112	        return {
113	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
114	            "stop": self.stop or None,
115	            "temperature": self.temperature,
116	            "top_p": self.top_p,
117	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
118	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
119	        }
120	
121	    def to_srt_kwargs(self):
122	        return {
123	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
124	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_new_tokens,
125	            "n": self.n,
126	            "stop": self.stop,
127	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
128	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
129	            "temperature": self.temperature,
130	            "top_p": self.top_p,
131	            "top_k": self.top_k,
132	            "min_p": self.min_p,
133	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
134	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
135	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
136	            "regex": self.regex,
137	            "json_schema": self.json_schema,
138	        }
